stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 3 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 8 observations with mean 29.9 /10 (within-chain SD = 7.9). chain B has 15 observations with mean 62.6 /10 (within-chain SD = 9.0). chain C has 20 observations with mean 33.0 /10 (within-chain SD = 3.2). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.9 and 19.8 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain C: 33.0 /10 (shrunk from raw 33.0). Range [33.0, 33.1] as between-chain SD varies from 7.9 to 19.8. Pooled mean: 42.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 42.7 /10 (weighted average of 3 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=13.2: B = 0.997.
Interval [33.0, 33.1] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (33.0) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (42.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
